query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "agentdog 1.5: a lightweight and scalable alignment framework for ai ag" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (d) as a foundational methodology/framework reference for "alignment frameworks" in AI, and criterion (a) by proposing a specific mechanism (bidirectional adaptation in multi-party loops) that extends the user's interest in alignment frameworks beyond the dyadic scope of the cited "AgentDoG" work.
